audit_items.proof_trace: In `timerHandler`, `data` is used (printf, L43) strictly before `RedisModule_Free(data)` (L44) with no subsequent access. The Redis module timer system fires each timer exactly once — `moduleTimerHandler` (module.c:4254-4257) removes the timer from the radix tree immediately after the callback returns, preventing re-invocation. Each loop iteration in `TimerCommand_RedisCommand` allocates a fresh `buf`, so no two timers share a pointer and no double-free is possible.
